name: Zeus Street Greek
challenge: Rapid franchise growth across 13+ separate legal entities, each needing its own bank account, Xero file and approval controls.
solution: Centralised multi-entity administration with deep Xero integration and multi-level approval workflows, piloted in a handful of new restaurants before full rollout.
proof: 13 entities onboarded, an expected +$760/month in revenue growth, and evaluated head-to-head against Airwallex on accounts payable functionality — Weel won the deal.

name: Grill'd
challenge: Keeping card control and spend visibility consistent across 200-300 restaurant staff nationwide, while feeding clean data into NetSuite and Power BI.
solution: A phased rollout with full NetSuite integration — single vendor import, GL coding — and commercial terms locked in at CFO level.
proof: 200-300 users rolled out across every location, with price stability guaranteed in perpetuity.

name: Ganley Group
challenge: Manual receipt chasing and reconciliation across 6 venues under one ownership group, made worse by EOFY pressure and a head-office move.
solution: Entity-by-entity onboarding — starting with a small pilot group including the General Manager — with Xero integration, auto-categorisation and receipt capture to cut manual admin.
proof: All 6 venues live on a $430/month multi-entity plan, rated ahead of competitor Budgetly during evaluation.

name: Yo-Chi
challenge: Physical card delivery reliability across multiple venue entities, with delays disrupting day-to-day operations at store level.
solution: Multi-venue card issuance backed by fast service recovery — fee waivers when delivery failed, rather than losing the relationship over an operational hiccup.
proof: 16 cards reissued across 2 entities, with $264 in fees waived as a goodwill and retention action.
